item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Proposal has been updated to include link to SCP-1761 and SCP-2771.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Renmar Citizenship League and Reseller Corporation also seems to be operating in the same weird market economy that Envelope Logistics®, Light Courier Enterprises, and Oneiroi things like SCP-2876 exist in. So there might be some links there.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I personally have always felt like these two are related, ever since the Short Works contest.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That's another good one.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: What about 2771?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: We could probably have links going both ways, between em.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: perhaps something to do with the Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to speak to Roget about this skip, and his headcanon regarding this extra-dimensional travel agent and his various patrons. According to Roget, since the instances of SCP-1362 are extra-dimensional beings, it would make sense that they may be spotted among 1526's customers. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

  • A human body which was entirely filled with carpeting fibers. Identified itself as "Juan" and had legal residency papers for the nation of Spain.
  • Human body was filled with 6,319 slug-like organisms of unknown composition. Was in possession of 6,319 temporary work visas for the nation of Austria. Work visas were found to be valid.
  • Internal structure was entirely mechanical. Appeared to have no legal residency documentation. Note that the subject approached by SCP-1526-A had received a sum of only 175 Norwegian kroner.
  • Subject was a plastic mannequin with residency documents for the nation of Indonesia taped to its buttocks. Believed to be related to SCP-1526 phenomenon due to materializing within 200 meters of a subject affected by SCP-1526.

To this:

  • A human body which was entirely filled with carpeting fibers. Identified itself as "Juan" and had legal residency papers for the nation of Spain.
  • Human body was filled with 6,319 slug-like organisms of unknown composition. Was in possession of 6,319 temporary work visas for the nation of Austria. Work visas were found to be valid.
  • Internal structure was entirely mechanical. Appeared to have no legal residency documentation. Note that the subject approached by SCP-1526-A had received a sum of only 175 Norwegian kroner.
  • Subject was a plastic mannequin with residency documents for the nation of Indonesia taped to its buttocks. Believed to be related to SCP-1526 phenomenon due to materializing within 200 meters of a subject affected by SCP-1526.
  • Humanoid whose upper body constantly produce a thick, black smoke, which obscured their face and upper torso. Believed to be an instance of SCP-1362.
  • An individual claiming to be a citizen of the Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams
  • A humanoid dressed in poorly-cut cloth approximation of black tactical gear and green uniform. Believed to be an instance of SCP-2771-1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one of those articles where the individual elements are okay-to-good but don't really gel together. The citizenship switch thing is fine, but not particularly interesting (although the woman tearing through the plane was inspired), while 1670 and whatever number the Automaton Parliment is did the whole "humanoid aliens with bizarre biology" schtick to much greater effect; here it just feels thrown in. And maybe it's just all the time I spent reading stuff at the Wanderers' Library, but the whole "documentation of extradimensional shenanigans done in a format similar to 'normal' documentation" isn't interesting on its own anymore, and this doesn't add anything.new to it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this explained?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this SCP need to be renamed or marked as explained for some reasons?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Has anything, Deci?

Has anything?

(My only regret at the moment is that the theme from Papers, Please is a march while the theme from Don't Starve is a waltz, making a mashup difficult.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has this stopped you before? :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suddenly I feel a need to cross-test this with SCP-2771.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Relax guys! All you have to do is not sell your citizenship(s) to people! If you heed these instructions, you'll be just fine!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, Papers, Please, did I ever enjoy playing that game.
Just had to mention this, man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glory to The Plane Where Eyes Cannot Follow, Open, Screaming Mouths

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My old stomping grounds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … why would the purchasing agent approach someone who didn't have anything to sell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: makes me wonder what happens if a person is approached who doesnt have any legal citizenship. i suppose the robot one might allude to it, but not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All instances of SCP-1526-1 are to be monitored by Foundation assets in their current locations. All attempts for these subjects to return to their place of origin are to be denied. Families and other associates are to be issued Class-C amnestics, and falsified records showing their move to their current location.

But "1526-1" is designation of those alien entities. Maybe you mean "all victims of SCP-1526-A are to be monitored… etc"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: eeeeeeeee unconditional praise from kalinin yay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Plane Where Eyes Cannot Follow, Open, Screaming Mouths

I wanna know more about this place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How on earth did I miss this one? This is great, upvoted.

item.title: Re: Birth of a Notion
item.content:encoded:
You know, I never thought about what it might be doing with the citizenships…

This might be worth an upvote after all.

item.title: Birth of a Notion
item.content:encoded:
+ IRC log from July 2013 (long)
- For anyone who's interested in how ideas are born

<Voct> there was something on the radio exploring the morality of selling your body parts. And it was pointed out that even the most extreme libertarians, the ones who do advocate that selling one's body parts be legal, typically dislike the notion of selling one's citizenship.
<Voct> which is an idea that could be used as the basis for an SCP
<Voct> what do you think?
<clone> I think that I have absolutely never heard that
<clone> and it could work yeah, but writing is not my thing
<clone> so I have no idea how it'd be done
<Voct> oh, I"m not saying you should write it
<Voct> I'm throwing the idea open
<Voct> an alien entity that approaches people and asks to buy their citizenship for a large sum of money
<Voct> and if they agree, they're instantaneously deported
<gumbal1> What kind of alien?
<Voct> and are physically unable to return to within the borders of their own nation
*clone is aware of that fact
<Voct> some sort of extradimensional thing, buying citizenship for otherworld refugees
<DreadLindwyrm> So this would render them stateless?
<Cain> That's good. Really good, Voct.
<DreadLindwyrm> What about dual-citizens?
<Voct> DreadLindwyrm - ah! That's the analogy!
<clone> DreadLindwyrm: Maybe they can't enter both nations?
<Voct> on the radio, it was people who sell one kidney, because they've got two
<Voct> so the purchasing agent would only approach people with dual citizenship
<Voct> because they can afford to lose one
<DreadLindwyrm> Nice.
<DreadLindwyrm> :D
<Voct> DreadLindwyrm - and it's always the *better* citizenship that's lost
<Voct> like, if you're a citizen of the US and of Syria
<Voct> POP you're back in Syria
<clone> Voct: So the SCP would be the agent?
<Voct> clone - yep.
<DreadLindwyrm> What about US/UK citizens?
<gumbal1> Would it take Canadian or US citizenship?
<Voct> hm
<Voct> actually
<gumbal1> How does it define the better one?
<Voct> it takes the citizenship of the nation with the larger population
<FlameShirt> Well, each one could have a price.
<Voct> more room for the refugee to blend in
<FlameShirt> There's various development metrics that could be used.
<FlameShirt> Human Development Index and the like.
<DreadLindwyrm> It's a nice idea. I assume the incoming aliens are close to human?
<Voct> DreadLindwyrm - we never see the refugees, only the purchasing agent.
<Voct> the purchasing agent resides squarely in Uncanny Valley
<clone> could it be that some of the refugees were other scps?
<Voct> that's not impossible
<Cain> What does the person get in exchange?
<Voct> Money.
<DreadLindwyrm> I was just thinking so as not to cause a fuss when the refugees arrive, it would help if they were "normal" humans.
<DreadLindwyrm> In the currency of the citizenship they lose? :p
<Voct> or at least *look* like normal humans
<Voct> oh, and because the agent is resettling refugees, it's kind of in a hurry
<gumbal1> DreadLindwyrm: That's an interesting situation
<Voct> so it's not quite 100% following all procedure exactly the way it should
<Cain> I don't know about that. It doesn't feel 'out there' enough.
<Cain> This is something that does happen.
<Voct> if you say anything that can be interpreted as "yes"
<Cain> Maybe on a larger scale.
<Cain> Planetary.
<Voct> Cain - what does?
<Cain> Galactic.
<Cain> Selling of rights.
<Voct> mm
<Voct> sell your citizenship
<Cain> Citizenship can be given away.
<Cain> Not legally, of course.
<clone> I feel like this could be a whole book on its own
<Voct> aha
<Cain> But identities.
Cain> Interesting, but right now your scip is just a teleporter.
<Cain> To another Earthly place.
<Cain> If it shot them to Quazar-Gamma-1834b127
<DreadLindwyrm> A skip that then prevents you from re-entering your former state.
<Voct> Cain - and the affected humans are physically unable to reenter the borders of their previous nation
<clone> Hey voct, maybe it could only take the nationality of people who were born from parents that came from different nations?
<gumbal1> What happens to the citizenship, though?
<Voct> gumbal1 - it gets reassigned to some desperate alien refugee
<gumbal1> Does it transfer to another unearthly being?
<Voct> gumbal1 - yes.
<clone> like if your parents are american and south african
<clone> 1 sec brb
<Cain> I get that, but it doesn't do much for me. I think it could be the same thing, but on a larger and more interesting scale.
<Voct> gumbal1 - the purchasing agent is buying citizenships from humans with dual citizenships, so that it can bestow these citizenships on alien refugees.
<Cain> With room for a much more interesting log.
<Voct> Cain - such as?
<gumbal1> But what type of aliens?
<Voct> we never see them
<gumbal1> Human ones, or slightly less terrestrial?
<Cain> So this… /guy/ gives somebody cash for their SS number and name.
<Voct> "of course they look human. It's important that they be able to blend in with their new neighbors!"
<Voct> yeah
<Cain> And then they get blorped into the third world.
<Voct> yep
<Cain> And can't come back.
<Voct> exactly
<Cain> What if they get shot into another dimension?
<gumbal1> And you could accidentally say yes
<Voct> gumbal1 - exactly. The purchasing agent is kind of a jerk that way.
<DreadLindwyrm> Extra dimension is boring.
<Cain> Or we /do/ get to see the new resident.
<Cain> Or WHATEVER.
<Voct> Cain - perhaps we already have.
<DreadLindwyrm> And we'd not be able to tell that from dead
<Cain> Another inhabited planet.
<gumbal1> I like this idea
<Voct> DreadLindwyrm - exactly. Other dimension, other planet ==> WHERE DID THEY GO? THEY'RE NOT HERE AND WE CAN'T FIND THEM!
<DreadLindwyrm> How would we, the foundation, know about that.
<gumbal1> Being stranded just for a poor choice of words
<clone> Yeah we could make some humanoid scps be connected to that guy
<Voct> the purchasing agent thinks it's being a hero
<gumbal1> Same way we found out about SCP-1897, I guess
<Cain> There's a lot that goes unexplained. It's a hole in the plot, admittedly.
<Cain> But it can be covered easily enough with redaction.
<ProcyonLotor> you should never cover a plot hole with redaction
<ProcyonLotor> that is lazy writing
<Voct> I don't think I'm up for writing this idea today
<clone> Cain: you could build on it with interview logs
<DreadLindwyrm> I think the "kicked out the country and can't re-enter" works better. There's an effect that draws our attention, rather than "random disappearance of people".
<Voct> would anyone like it?
<gumbal1> I would
<Voct> and you can't go into embassies or consulates
<Voct> because those count as your country's territory
<clone> I already like it
<clone> But how would they be unable to enter
<gumbal1> What if the country you reside in gets taken over by your home country?
<clone> would it be like one guy tried to cross into his country but got hit by a stray bullet
<clone> or *insert random convenient accident*
<Voct> normally, what happens is you try to cross the border, and a forcefield appears
<Voct> and it only blocks *you*
<Voct> which could be terrible if you're on an airplane
<Cain> I was just thinking that.
<DreadLindwyrm> Hm. What if it /looked like/ co-incidence - the plane you're on always has to divert for an emergency, or breaks down short of the border; the train derails near the border and so on? Less explicitly nasty, but the same effects?
<clone> DreadLindwyrm: Yeah I was thinking that would feel better than just a random forcefield
<DreadLindwyrm> If you push it, or you're alone, it might crash you - hard.
<Cain> I think that would make a good discovery story.
<clone> then again I love the idea of a person being squished alive by an invisible wall in a full airplane
<clone> imagine the company trying to explain it
<clone> It's just so funny
<Cain> 'SCP-XXXX was discovered when [a dude] exploded on a Southwest Airlines Northbound flight.
<DreadLindwyrm> Or, perhaps it starts off small, and escalates to the brick wall effect if you keep trying to bypass the deal.
<Voct> as for what happens if you've been deported from X to Y, and then X conquers Y (or Y conquers X): so far that hasn't happened, and the Foundation is not going to arrange international wars of conquest just to find out.
<clone> Wasn't there a nickelodeon show with the same principles
<clone> like there was a girl that couldn't go out of her hometown
<clone> for whatever reasons
<DreadLindwyrm> We could find out by diplomatic shifting of borders somewhere unimportant like the middle of a desert - redraw the line by a mile or so, and see how it changes things?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The bits after the bill of sale are redundant.

The description covers what happens when a person in a vehicle comes to a border, spelling out the airplane scenario is just gratuitous. And the last line literally adds nothing that wasn't covered by the Object Class header.

That space could be better used covering just what happens during an annexation. I have a mental image of naked diplomats shooting through the air at ludicrous speeds in the direction of the nearest uncontested real estate. It is equal parts hilarious and horrific.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am comforted that, of my dual citizenships, I would end up in the nation I prefer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. I don't think an interview would add enough to this article to justify its presence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh please no. Tired of SCPs which are fully detailed and explained in a interview at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, which ones (if you're cool with sharing)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first time having 3 citizenships has made something more scary to me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was at work gosh

Voct is credited with the idea for this. I forgot because I wrote this and then forgot about it for about 3 months.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ahem

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't do a whole lot for me and I feel like it's way uncontainable. I dunno, it's not sitting well, though I do like the line Anonolith points out above, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always had that question in my mind. The exact flgith scenario (almost by the letter). I gladly upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, but you need to be more clear on this.

1526-1 seems to be both the people displaced and the weird anomalous things that take their place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, I think Keter class would work better for this. It's pretty hard to track a global phenomenon like this and prevent all the people affected from going on airplanes or vehicles to that country.

Regardless, it's a great concept and good execution, I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this. Good work. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Plane Where Eyes Cannot Follow, Open, Screaming Mouths

Or, how to get an upvote in one phrase.
I really liked it, I was thinking about that airplane scenario and am glad you included it. Solid article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of Light Courier Enterprises, and I'm ok with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral until the Bill of Sale. Then I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this. I really, really like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted but I stand by my suggestion that this needs an interview.
